question: By what percentage did the tarsus length of birds decrease over time, and what environmental factor correlated with this change one year later?
answer: The tarsus length of birds decreased by 2.4 percent over time, and this change correlated with variations in summer temperatures occurring one year later.

video: The tarsus length of birds has decreased by 2.4 percent over time, as indicated by the data presented in the video.
text: Changes in summer temperature records correlated with changes in bird leg size occurring one year later.
